result:
Everything is set up. Here's a summary of what was created:

---

### Project Structure

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `requirements.txt` | Pinned deps: `plaid-python==38.4.0`, `flask==3.1.0` |
| `app.py` | Flask app with Plaid Link integration |

### Endpoints in `app.py`

| Route | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/create-link-token` | POST | Creates a Plaid Link token for your frontend to launch Link |
| `/api/exchange-token` | POST | Exchanges the public token from Link for a persistent `access_token` |
| `/api/webhook` | POST | Receives Plaid webhook events (transactions, item updates) |

### To Run

```bash
export PLAID_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id
export PLAID_SECRET=your_sandbox_secret
python app.py
```

The server starts on `http://localhost:5000`. Use Plaid's [Sandbox test credentials](https://plaid.com/docs/sandbox/) to test the flow.

> **Note:** The chub docs referenced version `25.1.0` of `plaid-python`, but that doesn't exist on PyPI Γאפ I pinned to `38.4.0` (the actual latest). The docs also recommended a `plaid-link-verify` package which doesn't exist as a real PyPI package, so I omitted it and used standard request validation instead.
